refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and enable targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link under representative conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance under agreed conditions.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange to support teleoperation.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.
DP2: Implement edge processing to reduce data volume and processing delay.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over network using QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.

coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates latency reduction in communication protocols, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol overhead increasing latency if not properly optimized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP2) if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle status data to teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and control inputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation latency logic.
Contains physical components but less suitable for communication protocol logic.
Highest containment score for teleoperation latency DP; low diffusion risk.
Primarily focused on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes latency optimization logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link performance outside Teleoperation module control.
Risk of interface mismatches if Teleoperation module does not fully encapsulate communication protocols.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Network Link and Vehicle modules to mitigate containment risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirement of minimizing communication latency with clear verification approach.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP1 is contained within Teleoperation module.
Interface dependencies on Network Link are well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module is the best architectural boundary for DP1, ensuring focused ownership and low diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency on Network Link interface and telemetry data flow.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functionality to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
Consider contingency plans if network conditions degrade beyond agreed parameters.

decision.justification: Functional decomposition and DP selection are sound, but further refinement of interface definitions and risk mitigation is needed before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance under agreed conditions.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange to support teleoperation.

revise.why: To clarify and strengthen interface contracts and containment boundaries to reduce risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Network link performance variability impacting latency.
Potential interface mismatches causing latency spikes.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface compliance tests between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
